hero.subtitle: Bistrot lyonnais de mere en fille
hero.description: Homemade family cooking since 1985 — 3 generations, generous portions, and the true taste of Lyon.
about.title: A Family Legacy in the Heart of Presqu'ile since 1985
about.p1: The story of Chez Mounier begins in 1983, when Marc Mounier opened "Chez les Gones" at the famous Halles de Lyon-Paul Bocuse. It was there that he invented the Gnafron, a sausage prepared in a cream and grated cheese mixture — a recipe that has since become a house classic. Two years later, in 1985, he settled at 3 Rue des Marronniers, in the heart of the Presqu'ile, and founded the restaurant that bears his name.
about.p2: Mme Mounier, the mother, welcomed guests for many years, sharing her expertise and the warm hospitality that Lyon's famous 'meres' were known for. Today, it is Chrystelle Mounier, her daughter, who runs the show. A true bouchon personality, she excels both at delighting stomachs and regaling diners with stories of Lyonnais gastronomy. And it is Manon, her granddaughter and the third generation, who completes the family picture by managing the dining room service.
about.h5_homemade: Everything Homemade, Even the Pastry Dough
about.p3: Here, you don't come for four-star gastronomy or visual artistry. You come for the pleasure of a good meal with real flavour. No pretension at Chez Mounier, just familiarity with guests. You arrive relaxed, leave with a full belly and a smile. Everything is homemade — from the tart pastry to the sauces — with fresh products selected from our local producers.
about.p4: The experience begins at the entrance, along the century-old bar. Soft banquettes line walls adorned with wood panelling. Everything is dressed in warm burgundy — from the tablecloths to the paintings, down to the trim of the plates. The mosaic floor gives the place a retro charm, that of a true old-time bistrot where time seems to have stood still.
specialties.title: Our Lyonnais Specialties
specialties.intro: The menu at Chez Mounier is a tribute to the Lyonnais culinary tradition. Every dish is prepared on-site with fresh products selected from our regional producers. The Gnafron — sausage in a cream and grated cheese mixture, invented by Marc Mounier at the Halles Bocuse — remains our most iconic dish. The quenelles de brochet sauce Nantua, incredibly generous, guarantee a wonderful evening. The tablier de sapeur with its golden breadcrumb coating never stops delighting, served with homemade sauce gribiche.
specialties.h5_value: Full Menu Under 20 Euros: the Lyonnais Value
specialties.value_text: Our full menus — starter, main course, cheese and dessert — start at under twenty euros. The Saint-Marcellin from Mere Richard, aged at the Halles de Lyon, sits alongside the cervelle de canut with fresh herbs. And to finish, the tarte aux pralines roses, with homemade shortcrust pastry, remains a must. All accompanied by beaujolais wines from our small producers, served in a traditional pot lyonnais.
rue.title: Rue des Marronniers: Cradle of the Bouchons Lyonnais
rue.h5_history: From Rue de Jerusalem to Rue des Marronniers
rue.p1: Built between 1715 and 1723, first named rue de Jerusalem then rue Neuves-des-Brayes, the Rue des Marronniers owes its current name to the chestnut trees that once lined the east side of Place Bellecour. At 120 metres long and 8 metres wide, this pedestrian street in the Presqu'ile has become over the decades one of the great hubs of Lyonnais gastronomy, with some twenty restaurants side by side.
rue.p2: Lyon is the world capital of gastronomy, and the Rue des Marronniers is its living symbol. It is here that the tradition of the bouchons endures — those family-run bistros where you eat well, simply, generously. But over the years, many establishments have given in to tourist temptation, adapting their menus and prices to the flow of visitors.
rue.p3: Chez Mounier stands as the last holdout. Since 1985, our bouchon lyonnais has stayed the course: authentic cooking, honest prices, generous portions and a family welcome. No English menu on the pavement, no touts at the door — just the quality of homemade food and the word-of-mouth of those in the know. That is why we are full every night.

cta.title: Full Every Night — Reserve by Phone
cta.description: Chez Mounier is always packed. To be sure of getting your table at our bouchon lyonnais, there is only one thing to do: call us. Tuesday to Saturday, lunch and dinner, our team will be delighted to welcome you at 3 Rue des Marronniers, steps from Place Bellecour.
footer.description: One of the last true bouchons on Rue des Marronniers. Homemade family cooking, 3 generations, generous portions and the true taste of Lyon.
footer.rights: Restaurant Chez Mounier. All rights reserved.

reviews.r1_text: A real bouchon like there are almost none left! The quenelles de brochet with Nantua sauce are absolutely divine, homemade and served beautifully gratinated. The family atmosphere with three generations in the kitchen — you can tell it's the real deal.
reviews.r2_text: Tablier de sapeur perfectly breaded and crispy, impeccable gribiche sauce. The portions are very generous for the price. Only small downside: the wait on a Saturday night, but it's worth it.
reviews.r3_text: The salade lyonnaise is a delight — crispy lardons, runny poached egg, perfect vinaigrette. We followed it with the tarte aux pralines roses, pure bliss. Unbeatable value for money in Lyon.
reviews.r4_text: The last true bouchon in the Marronniers neighbourhood. The gnafron is delicious, everything is homemade. Special mention for the beaujolais served in a pot. Warm and friendly atmosphere, we always come back.
reviews.r5_text: The œufs vigneronne as starter were delicious, very flavourful. The full menu — starter, main, cheese and dessert — for under €20 is exceptional value. Quick and friendly service.
reviews.r6_text: Three generations of expertise, you can taste it in every dish. The quenelles are the best in Lyon — generous portions and perfect cooking. Authentic family atmosphere with checkered tablecloths and the pot lyonnais, it feels like home.
